original_reasoning: Arabic womb can plausibly extend to a pouch, cavity, or enclosing vessel, giving a workable conceptual path.
The masdaq of flowing aligns plausibly with an onset, rush, or impulse.
حنث often concerns breaking what was sworn or said, so there is a real bridge from oath-bound speech to things said.
A harsh barren year naturally maps to distress and suffering, giving a solid hardship-to-pain chain.
تحبب 'become grainy' points to clustered abundance, which plausibly matches 'swarming with fish'.
Wrath, offense, and abusive speech lie in one moral-emotive field, so a conceptual drift is plausible.
Arabic زاج is stirring people against each other, while Greek Zeugma is a yoking/joining notion behind the place-name; both involve bringing sides into connection.
Both sides can be read as preserved name-material rather than lexical meaning; onomastic continuity is plausible.
